evals.prompt: i wanna add CI to this repo so every push and PR runs the linter and the tests, can you set that up
evals.expected_output: Detects the repo's language stack(s) from package.json or pyproject.toml. Writes .github/workflows/lint-and-test.yml on a branch named claude/ci-demo with one job per detected stack. Commits and pushes to that branch, opens a PR back to the default branch (or reuses an existing one). Final JSON has skill='lint-and-test', status='created' on first run, populated commit_url and pr_url, and workflow_runs containing at least one entry with a queued/in_progress run.

evals.prompt: set up the same lint+test CI as before — should be a no-op since I already asked
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing .github/workflows/lint-and-test.yml and runs the python3 -c yaml semantic compare against the rendered template. Output is SAME, so the skill exits without writing, committing, or pushing. Final JSON has status='no_change', commit_url is the previous commit (looked up via git log), pr_url is the existing PR, workflow_runs may be empty.
